verses.commentary: Crowd of many thousands gathered, trampling each other. Jesus addresses disciples first: 'Beware of the leaven (zyme, ζύμη) of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.' Diagnostic identification of the leaven.
'Nothing is covered up that will not be revealed, or hidden that will not be known.' Eschatological disclosure as motivation for sincerity now.
What you have said in the dark will be heard in the light; whispered in private rooms — proclaimed on the housetops. Words have eventual destinies.
'I tell you my friends, do not fear those who kill the body and after that have nothing more they can do.' Friends (philoi, φίλοι) — intimate address.
'I will warn you whom to fear: fear him who, after he has killed, has authority to cast into hell. Yes, I tell you, fear him.' Eternal authority outranks temporal.
Five sparrows for two pennies — not one is forgotten before God. Even the cheapest are remembered.
The hairs of your head are all numbered. Do not fear — you are of more value than many sparrows. Providence is granular.
'Whoever acknowledges me before men — the Son of Man will acknowledge before the angels of God.' Public-private symmetry.
Denial inverted: denied before the angels of God. Reciprocal.
Speaking against the Son of Man — forgiven. Blasphemy against the Holy Spirit — not forgiven. The Spirit-blasphemy unique unforgivable.
When brought before synagogues, rulers, authorities — do not be anxious how to defend yourselves or what to say.
The Holy Spirit will teach you in that very hour what you ought to say. Spirit-given defense.
Someone in the crowd: 'Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.' Family financial dispute brought to Jesus.
'Man, who made me a judge or arbitrator (kriten e meristen, κριτὴν ἢ μεριστὴν) over you?' Declines the role. Not his function.
'Take care, be on guard against all covetousness (pleonexia, πλεονεξία — greed for more) — one's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.' Life-definition corrected.
Parable: the land of a rich man produced abundantly. The good harvest is itself the test.
He thinks to himself: 'What shall I do? I have nowhere to store my crops.' Surplus problem — interior monologue, no consultation with others.
'I will tear down my barns and build larger; I will store all my grain and my goods.' Self-focused solution.
'I will say to my soul, Soul, you have ample goods laid up for many years. Relax, eat, drink, be merry.' Hedonic plan.
'But God said: Fool! This night your soul is required of you. The things you have prepared — whose will they be?' Sudden interruption. The accumulator dies the night of completion.
'So is the one who lays up treasure for himself and is not rich toward God.' Two wealth-types contrasted.
To disciples: 'Therefore do not be anxious about your life — what you will eat — or about your body — what you will wear.' Echoes Sermon on Mount.
Life is more than food; the body more than clothing. The bigger truth eclipses the smaller anxiety.

Consider the ravens (Lukan distinctive — Matthew has 'birds'; ravens were considered unclean in Leviticus 11, yet God feeds them). They neither sow nor reap, no storehouse, no barn — God feeds them. How much more you?
Who by being anxious can add a single hour (or cubit) to his life? Anxiety produces no actual increase.
If you cannot do even the smallest, why be anxious about the rest? Argument from lesser to greater.
Consider the lilies — they neither toil nor spin — yet Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these. Beauty without effort.
If God so clothes the grass — alive today, tomorrow thrown into the oven — how much more you, O of little faith. The fortiori argument.
Do not seek what you are to eat and drink — nor be of anxious mind. Anxiety as orientation, not just emotion.
The nations of the world seek these things — your Father knows you need them. Gentile-pattern contrasted with Father-child relation.
Seek his kingdom — these things will be added. Priority sequence.
'Fear not, little flock (mikron poimnion, μικρὸν ποίμνιον), for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.' Tender address. The kingdom is gift.
Sell your possessions, give to the needy. Provide yourselves with moneybags that do not grow old, treasure in heaven that does not fail. Investment relocated.
Where your treasure is, your heart will be also. Heart follows resources.
'Stay dressed for action, keep your lamps burning' — service-readiness imagery.
Be like men waiting for their master to come home from the wedding feast — to open the door immediately when he knocks.

Blessed are those servants whom the master finds awake. 'Truly I tell you, he will dress himself for service, have them recline at table, and come and serve them.' Reversal — master serves the watchful. Foreshadows the foot-washing.
If he comes in the second watch (9pm-midnight) or the third (midnight-3am) and finds them so — blessed are those servants. The blessing extends regardless of hour.
If the master of the house had known at what hour the thief was coming, he would not have left it broken into. Vigilance is rational.
'You also must be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.' Preparedness is the response to unknown timing.
Peter: 'Lord, are you telling this parable for us, or for all?' Audience clarification requested.
Who then is the faithful and wise manager (oikonomos, οἰκονόμος) — put in charge to give portions of food at the proper time? Leadership question framed.
Blessed is that servant whom his master finds doing so when he comes.
Truly I tell you, he will set him over all his possessions. Faithful management at small scale earns expansion.
If that servant says, 'My master is delayed' — begins to beat the male and female servants, eat and drink and get drunk. Delay misused as license.
Master will come on a day he does not expect, hour he does not know — cut him in pieces (dichotomesei, διχοτομήσει — severe judgment image), put him with the unfaithful.
The servant who knew his master's will and did not get ready or act — will receive a severe beating.
The one who did not know and did things deserving a beating — will receive a light beating. To whom much is given, of him much will be required. Differentiated accountability based on knowledge.
'I came to cast fire on the earth — and would that it were already kindled.' Eschatological fire of judgment and purification both.
'I have a baptism (baptisma, βάπτισμα) to be baptized with, and how distressed (synechomai, συνέχομαι — pressed) I am until it is accomplished.' His own death framed as baptism.
'Do you think I came to give peace on earth? No, I tell you — but rather division.' Same as Matthew 10:34's 'sword.'
From now on five in one house divided — three against two and two against three. Family fracture.

Father against son, son against father; mother against daughter, daughter against mother; mother-in-law against daughter-in-law, daughter-in-law against mother-in-law. Three generations split along the Jesus-line.
To the crowd: 'When you see a cloud rising in the west, you say at once, A shower is coming — and so it happens.' Mediterranean weather pattern.
When the south wind blows, you say there will be scorching heat — and it happens. Reliable predictions.
'Hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of earth and sky, but how is it you do not interpret the present time?' Theological meteorology failed.
'Why do you not judge for yourselves what is right?' Discernment demanded.

When you go with your accuser before the magistrate — make an effort to settle on the way. Otherwise he drags you to the judge, and the judge hands you to the officer, and the officer puts you in prison.
'You will never get out until you have paid the very last lepton (smallest coin).' Eschatological warning: settle accounts with God now, before judgment forecloses the option.
